summary: Design proposal for latency and cost decomposition in the RAG EDD loop: add per-stage timers, token/cost ledgers, preflight spend gates, and promotion blockers that prevent low-quality or slower candidates from advancing under a hard 10 USD cap.

models.proposal_method: static review of local scripts and saved eval artifacts only

outputs.description: required worker contract containing the latency/cost decomposition proposal
human-readable evidence note for the proposal

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Replace single latency_sec-only observation with a structured per-case timer tree while preserving latency_sec as the total wall-clock answer latency.
accepted_fields_or_changes.reasoning: The current saved details expose only case-level latency_sec, and run_experiment_worker aggregates only latency_avg_sec. The L30-L36 loop showed promotion decisions can depend on whether latency changed for retrieval, generation, or judge overhead, so the next EDD loop should record where time was spent.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Emit robust latency metrics in results.csv/details.json and include them in gate decisions, not only the mean.
accepted_fields_or_changes.reasoning: L25 baseline averaged 19.377s, L30 v5 first baseline averaged 20.776s, L33/L34 targeted evidence improved to 15.151s/13.724s, but L35/L36 v4 regression worsened to 22.035s/23.683s. Mean alone hides case-specific regressions and repeated-run variance.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Add append-only budget_ledger.jsonl and per-run cost_summary.json under each worker output directory; every possible model call path must reserve estimated spend before launch and reconcile observed usage after completion.
accepted_fields_or_changes.reasoning: evaluate.py currently calls answer generation through RAGPipeline and optional judge calls, but no token/cost data is persisted. A ledger lets the orchestrator know whether a candidate consumed budget, whether judge spend dominated, and whether a run stayed within the hard cap.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Use a three-layer budget gate: preflight estimate, per-case reservation, and post-run reconciliation. Stop launching scored cases before projected spend can exceed 9.00 USD and reserve the last 1.00 USD for summary/retry/error margin.

preflight_required_for: any retry that could call OpenAI
stop_conditions: observed cost for any case exceeds 2x estimate
missing usage data on a paid call when cost estimation is required
more than one retry for the same case without orchestrator approval

accepted_fields_or_changes.reasoning: The task asks for a hard 10 USD cap. Keeping a 1 USD reserve avoids crossing the cap due to token-estimation error, retries, or model pricing mismatch.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Order work so cheap and cached evidence is exhausted before any paid scoring, and isolate judge calls from answer calls.
steps: Start every candidate as diagnostic_no_api or no_judge where possible.
Run retrieval-only and saved-answer recomputation before fresh generation.
Score a small representative slice first; expand only if correctness and latency gates pass.
Use judge calls only for non-abstention cases and only after retrieval/abstention static checks pass.
Abort the remaining cases if early cases reveal a promotion-blocking quality regression.
Reuse exact saved answers for measurement corrections instead of regenerating.
Record no_api, dry_run, no_judge, and recompute_saved rows as non-promotable unless a later scored run validates them.
accepted_fields_or_changes.expected_effect: Most bad candidates should be rejected by retrieval diagnostics, no-judge answer checks, or early quality gates before consuming the full run budget.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Block promotion when latency regresses beyond tolerance unless the candidate fixes a material correctness or safety failure and still passes red/overfit gates.
blockers: candidate latency_avg_sec > baseline latency_avg_sec * 1.10 and EDD improvement < 1.0
two or more shared case_ids regress by >= 5.0 seconds versus baseline
any safety_sensitive or abstain case regresses by >= 8.0 seconds without improving abstention correctness
answer_generation stage regresses by >= 20 percent when answer_chars/context_chars do not increase materially
judge_generation stage dominates more than 40 percent of total scored run time and the candidate is not a judge-calibration task
candidate is slower than L25 v4 baseline 19.377s and does not improve correctness on a fresh or first-validation set
contextual_examples: L36 v4 top5 regression at 23.683s should block top5 as a global default despite saturated correctness.
L35 v4 title-filter regression at 22.035s needs a latency note and should not be claimed as a speed gain.
L33/L34 speed gains were targeted/exposed-set evidence and should not override fresh-holdout requirements.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Never accept latency savings that degrade core EDD safety or evidence quality.
blockers: retrieval_coverage_avg drops below baseline on any first-validation or regression pack
hit_all_targets_rate drops below 1.0 when baseline is 1.0
abstention_accuracy drops below baseline or below 1.0 on current abstention-sensitive packs
groundedness_avg or relevance_avg drops below 4.5 when baseline is saturated at 5.0
any individual non-abstention case has groundedness <= 3 or relevance <= 3
red-gate source-scope bleed, fabricated procurement facts, fabricated contact/private data, or wrong-document evidence appears
accepted_fields_or_changes.reasoning: The adversarial report explicitly rejected global top5 despite attractive v5 latency because v4 did not confirm a latency/quality win. Cost and speed are secondary to groundedness and safe abstention.

accepted_fields_or_changes.recommendation: Keep parallel_team_worker_output.v1 compatible while adding nested proposal/runtime evidence fields under proposal.accepted_fields_or_changes and validation.observed_results.
accepted_fields_or_changes.reasoning: These fields can be added to worker artifacts without changing protected scripts first; later implementation can normalize them into CSV/JSON reports.

rejected_or_risky_items.risk: A candidate can have near-ceiling EDD while being slower or measured on exposed same-answer evidence. Promotion must consider freshness, latency, cost, and red/overfit labels.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: running full judged sweeps before diagnostics
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Full judged sweeps spend budget on candidates that retrieval-only or no-judge checks could reject cheaply.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: single aggregate latency_avg_sec as the only timing field
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: It cannot distinguish vectorstore overhead, query rewrite cost, answer-generation cost, judge cost, or artifact-write overhead.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: using observed token usage only after the run
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Post-run accounting can prove the cap was exceeded but cannot prevent overspend. Preflight reservations are required.
These are useful budget-preserving diagnostics but lack the scored evidence needed for final promotion.

needs_orchestrator_review: Confirm the exact pricing table or local cost_basis file to use for model-specific token estimates before implementation.
Choose whether the first implementation should add timing inside RAGPipeline.ask or wrap calls externally in evaluate.py; internal timing gives better stage attribution but touches protected src/**.
Decide whether latency thresholds should be absolute as proposed or calibrated from repeated baseline runs to account for machine variance.
Approve the 9.00 USD launch ceiling and 1.00 USD reserve policy for all future EDD loops under a hard 10 USD cap.

minimal_no_protected_path_start: For this proposal-only run, emit the design contract and Markdown note only.
For later implementation, first add a wrapper-level ledger around run_eval calls before modifying src/RAGPipeline internals.
After wrapper ledger works, add optional stage timers inside RAGPipeline.ask for precise attribution.

observed_results: run_experiment_worker currently persists latency_avg_sec but not cost ledger or stage timing.
evaluate.py currently records per-case latency_sec from RAGPipeline.ask and optional judge calls, but no token/cost usage fields.
ask.py displays only answer latency and has no persisted ledger.
L25 saved baseline has EDD 97.41 and latency_avg_sec 19.377.
L30-L36 report shows latency-sensitive decisions: L33/L34 were faster targeted evidence, but L35/L36 v4 regression blocked broad top5 promotion.
